blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเชี่ยวชาญ Delivery Room Resuscitation?
การเปลี่ยนผ่านจาก fetus → neonate ใช้เวลาเพียงไม่กี่นาที แต่ failure ใน transition นั้นทำให้เกิด death หรือ lifelong neurologic injury ได้. (1) ~10% ของ births ต้องการ active intervention; ~3% ต้องการ PPV; ~1–2/1000 ต้องการ full resuscitation (chest compressions/epi); (2) Ventilation = cornerstone ของ neonatal resuscitation — ต่างจาก adult ACLS ที่เน้น circulation; (3) Primary vs secondary apnea: แยกไม่ได้จาก clinical appearance → ต้อง assume secondary apnea และ resuscitate ทันที; (4) Room air (21%) สำหรับ ≥35 wk; 21–30% สำหรับ <35 wk — hyperoxia kills preterm brain; (5) Admission temperature <36.5°C ใน VLBW → 28% ↑ mortality per 1°C decrease (Laptook 2007) — thermal management ไม่ใช่ optional. การ resuscitate ที่ห้อง delivery คือ neonatology ที่ pure ที่สุด.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
อธิบาย fetal circulation และกระบวนการ transition at birth: ductus venosus, foramen ovale, ductus arteriosus — ปิดอย่างไร
ระบุ causes of neonatal depression ทั้ง antepartum และ intrapartum risk factors (Box 30.1)
อธิบาย asphyxial cascade: diving reflex, redistribution of blood flow, primary apnea → secondary apnea (Fig 30.4)
ระบุ HANDS mnemonic ในการสื่อสาร resuscitation team call; องค์ประกอบ preparation: equipment, personnel, simulation
ใช้ NRP 2020 algorithm (Fig 30.6): initial assessment → initial steps → PPV → ETT → chest compressions → epinephrine
อธิบาย initial steps NRP 8th ed: warm, dry, stimulate, position (sniff position); เมื่อไหร่ NOT to suction
ระบุ thermal management strategies ตาม gestational age: plastic wrap, exothermic mattress, delivery room temperature
ใช้ oxygen titration table: target SpO2 ตาม minute-of-life (1–10 นาที); initial FiO2 ตาม GA; เมื่อไหร่ใช้ 100%
อธิบาย Apgar score: 5 criteria, timing (1/5/10/15/20 min), ข้อจำกัด; umbilical cord blood gas interpretation
ระบุ ethical/legal framework: AMA code, Child Abuse Amendments 1984, periviable counseling; เมื่อไหร่ withhold resuscitation
1. Fetal Physiology — Circulation and Transition
1.1 Fetal Circulation
Fetal PaO2: 20–30 mm Hg — แต่ tissues ไม่ hypoxic เพราะ adaptive mechanisms 3 อย่าง:
(a) Fetal Hb (HbF): oxyhemoglobin curve shift ซ้าย → ↑ O2 affinity → ↑ saturation ที่ low PO2 เดียวกัน
(b) Higher cardiac output: fetal tissues perfused at higher rate than adult → compensates low SaO2
(c) Low fetal O2 demand: ไม่ต้อง thermoregulate + respiratory effort minimal → O2 consumption ต่ำ
Umbilical venous blood (returning from placenta): PO2 20–40 mm Hg (can be up to 45 mmHg by percutaneous sampling)
Umbilical arterial blood (going back to placenta): PO2 15–25 mm Hg
Placenta = lowest resistance organ → receives ~40% of fetal cardiac output
1.2 Fetal Shunts — The Three Bypass Circuits

rows: Bypasses portal circulation: oxygenated umbilical venous blood → IVC → foramen ovale → left heart → coronary/carotid arteries
Loss of umbilical flow at cord clamping → functional closure; ligamentum venosum
Right atrium → left atrium: directs most-oxygenated blood (from IVC stream, crista dividens) to left heart
↑ Left atrial pressure > right atrial pressure when pulmonary venous return ↑ after ventilation → flap closes
Right ventricle → descending aorta (bypasses lungs): ~90% of RV output
↑ PaO2 after ventilation + ↓ prostaglandins → constriction; patent in fetus due to low PO2 + prostaglandins

blocks.text: 1.3 Transition at Birth — The Three-Phase Process (Hooper 2016)

Respiratory Transition — Three Phases
Phase 1 — Lung fluid clearance: Na+ reabsorption (active transport) drives fluid from alveolar space → interstitium; starts before labor, accelerates with catecholamines + inspiratory pressure gradients. Phase 2 — FRC establishment: first breaths create pressure gradients; peak inspiratory pressure −50 to −100 cmH2O possible; gas enters lung at very low pressures (<−5 cmH2O). Phase 3 — PVR decline: ventilation → ↑ PaO2 + ↓ PCO2 + ↑ pH → ↓ PVR → ↑ pulmonary blood flow → ↑ LA return → LA pressure > RA → foramen ovale closes. Note: full aeration within 3–5 breaths in term newborns. Na+ reabsorption NOT active in preterm → harder transition.
Delayed cord clamping (DCC) 30–60 sec: AAP/ACOG recommendation for vigorous term and preterm — ↑ blood volume, ↓ inotropic need, ↓ IVH, ↓ NEC (ACOG CO 684, 2017)
Exception: intact placental circulation required for DCC; if abruptio placentae or immediate resuscitation needed → immediate cord clamping
Sustained lung inflation (SLI) — NOT recommended for preterm: SAIL trial (Kirpalani JAMA 2019, <26 wk): stopped early due to ↑ mortality in SLI group
Oxygen and PVR: Lakshminrusimha Pediatr Res 2007 (lambs): air resuscitation ↓ PVR 72% at 30 min → equivalent to 100% O2 at 60–90 min —
O2 NOT essential to decrease PVR; mechanical ventilation alone sufficient early
Labor acidosis: term fetus enters labor at base excess −2 mmol/L → loses additional 3–4 mmol/L during uncomplicated vaginal delivery → normal transition not compromised
2. Causes of Neonatal Depression and Asphyxia
2.1 Antepartum Risk Factors
Maternal: diabetes, chronic hypertension, pregnancy-induced hypertension, cardiac/thyroid/pulmonary/renal illness, anemia/isoimmunization, substance abuse
Pregnancy: polyhydramnios, oligohydramnios, PPROM, post-term, multiple gestation, size-dates discrepancy, fetal malformation, ↓ fetal activity, no prenatal care
Previous: fetal or neonatal death; bleeding 2nd/3rd trimester; maternal infection
Medications: lithium carbonate, magnesium, adrenergic blocking drugs, SSRIs; maternal age <16 or >35 yr
2.2 Intrapartum Risk Factors
Emergency CS, forceps/vacuum-assisted delivery, breech/abnormal presentation
Premature labor, precipitous labor, prolonged labor (>24h), prolonged 2nd stage (>3h)
Chorioamnionitis, PROM >18h, uterine tetany
Fetal bradycardia, nonreassuring fetal heart rate patterns, general anesthesia
Narcotics to mother within 4h of delivery; meconium-stained AF; prolapsed cord; abruptio placentae; placenta previa

HANDS Mnemonic — สื่อสาร Neonatal Team Call
H = Hemorrhage (เลือดออก?); A = Amniotic fluid (clear or meconium-stained?); N = Number of infants expected (twin/triplet?); D = Dates (gestational age?); S = Fetal monitoring Strip (Category I/II/III?). ข้อมูลเหล่านี้กำหนด: team composition, equipment prep, cord management plan, parental counseling ก่อน delivery.
3. Response to Asphyxia — Physiologic Cascade
3.1 Types of Intrapartum Asphyxia

Acute near-total asphyxia: abrupt onset 5–30 min; complete/near-complete cessation of fetal blood flow. Causes: maternal cardiac arrest, uterine rupture, complete placental abruption, severe cord compression. No time for diving reflex — less multisystem organ damage from redistribution

Partial prolonged asphyxia: slow onset over hours; uteroplacental insufficiency. Classic 'diving reflex' occurs: hypoxia + acidosis → ↑ PVR → right-to-left shunt → blood away from periphery → preserved perfusion to brain/heart/adrenals
3.2 Cardiovascular Redistribution
Hypoxia/acidosis → ↑ noncerebral peripheral resistance → ↑ blood flow to head, heart, adrenals; ↓ flow to kidneys, liver, gut, bone
Blood pressure maintained as long as myocardium compensates; as asphyxia progresses → myocardial failure → ↓ cardiac output → ↓ BP
Hypoxic cardiomyopathy = intermediary step before brain/organ damage in partial prolonged asphyxia
3.3 Respiratory Pattern in Asphyxia

Primary Apnea vs Secondary Apnea — ทำไมต้อง Assume Secondary เสมอ
Asphyxial sequence: Gasping → Primary apnea (หยุด spontaneously, responds to stimulation) → Irregular gasping → Secondary apnea (no spontaneous recovery, requires PPV). KEY: ไม่สามารถแยก primary vs secondary apnea ได้จาก clinical appearance ณ เวลาที่เห็น. Therefore: ทุก depressed neonate ต้อง assume secondary apnea → initiate resuscitation ทันที โดยไม่รอดูว่าจะตอบสนองต่อ stimulation หรือไม่. ยิ่งอยู่ใน secondary apnea นาน → นานขึ้น ก่อนจะเริ่ม spontaneous ventilation หลัง PPV (Adamsons 1964).
Primary apnea: ↑ HR + gasping/breathing begins with stimulation; associated with early asphyxia
Secondary apnea: ↓↓ HR, severe metabolic acidemia; requires PPV for recovery — every minute delay → longer time to first spontaneous gasp
Clinical rule (Goldsmith): 'Apgar 1 min = 0–3 → often secondary apnea.' Don't wait for 1-min Apgar to initiate resuscitation
4. Preparation for Resuscitation
4.1 Equipment Checklist
Radiant warmer (preheated), blended O2 source with flowmeter, suction apparatus (bulb syringe + wall suction 80–100 mmHg)
PPV equipment: T-piece resuscitator OR self-inflating bag-mask; face masks (term + preterm sizes)
Intubation: laryngoscope + blades (0, 1), ETT sizes 2.5/3.0/3.5/4.0, stylet, CO2 detector
Monitoring: pulse oximeter + sensor, ECG leads + monitor (for anticipated difficult resuscitation)
Medications (draw up in advance for anticipated severe resuscitation): epinephrine 1:10,000, normal saline, umbilical catheter kit, volume expander
Plastic wrap + exothermic mattress + hat for ELBW infants (<1000 g)
All equipment: check before EVERY anticipated resuscitation (laryngoscope light, ETT cuff, O2 flow)
4.2 Personnel Requirements
AHA/AAP guideline: 'At every delivery, ≥1 person whose PRIMARY responsibility is the infant and who can initiate resuscitation including PPV'
Full resuscitation: ≥2–3 skilled providers needed (1 ventilates, 1 monitors HR/SpO2, 1 prepares medications/UVC)
NRP (Neonatal Resuscitation Program): AAP/AHA, 8th edition 2020 — starting point only, NOT a competency certificate
Skill decay is real: NRP recommends training frequency > every 2 years for retention. AAP + RQI Partners: self-directed high-frequency simulation program (short-interval practice)
Small-volume delivery centers (<500 deliveries/year): enhanced anticipation + planning required; unexpected adverse outcomes in up to 5% of low-risk newborns even at term
4.3 Simulation and Team Training
Simulation training: ↑ teamwork + communication in emergent situations (Murphy & Halamek NeoReviews 2005; Sawyer Adv Med Educ Pract 2016)
High-fidelity manikins: better anatomic fidelity but expensive; 'golden hour' scripted protocols for ELBW delivery
Mock code drills + video review of real resuscitations → ↓ tension, ↑ coordination, identifies errors
Thomas EJ J Perinatol 2006: teamwork quality correlated with delivery room outcomes — communication breakdown = major failure mode
5. NRP 2020 Algorithm — Overview

NRP 8th Edition 2020 — Decision Tree สรุป
Step A — Initial Assessment: Term? Good tone? Breathing/crying? YES → skin-to-skin + routine care. NO → warm, dry, stimulate, position airway (sniff position); suction if needed. Step B — At 1 min: Apnea/gasping/HR <100? → PPV + pulse oximeter + consider cardiac monitor. Labored breathing/persistent cyanosis only? → position airway, consider CPAP, O2. Step C: HR <100 after PPV? → Ensure adequate ventilation → consider ETT or laryngeal mask. Step D: HR <60? → ETT/laryngeal mask + chest compressions + 100% O2 + UVC. Step E: HR still <60? → IV epinephrine q3–5 min; consider hypovolemia (NS 10 mL/kg); consider pneumothorax (transillumination/emergency needle decompression).
6. Initial Steps — Warm, Dry, Stimulate, Position
6.1 Initial Quick Overview — Triage at Birth

Term + good tone + breathing/crying: → hand to mother immediately for skin-to-skin; no suctioning needed; light blanket to prevent evaporative heat loss while observing; head turned to one side, nose/mouth visible
NOT term, OR not breathing easily, OR diminished tone, OR not vigorous: → radiant warmer for detailed assessment; begin initial steps
Median HR in healthy term/preterm at birth: <100 bpm; may take up to 2 min to rise to 140 bpm → do not over-respond to initial low HR
Color assessment at birth: NOT helpful — even healthy term infant may take several minutes to achieve SpO2 >90%; blow-by oxygen NOT indicated for transient cyanosis
6.2 Initial Steps (NRP 8th ed — Reordered to Reflect Usual Practice)
Warm: radiant warmer at 100%; preheated for ELBW
Dry: unless very preterm → use plastic wrap instead; remove wet blankets immediately (evaporative heat loss)
Stimulate: drying is usually sufficient; additional: flick soles of feet or rub back;
Continued/more vigorous stimulation NOT useful if ineffective; allow hypoxia to persist
Position airway: 'sniffing position' — neck slightly extended; can use shoulder roll

Suction if needed: bulb syringe mouth THEN nose ('m before n'); NOT routine for vigorous babies born through clear fluid (Foster Cochrane 2017; Gungor Gynecol Obstet Invest 2006). Posterior pharyngeal stimulation → vagal bradycardia (Cordero 1971)
6.3 Post-Stimulation Decision
After initial steps: evaluate HR + respiratory effort → primary decision point
If gasping or respirations insufficient to maintain HR >100 bpm → initiate PPV immediately
PPV for ≥35 wk: start with 21% FiO2 (room air); <35 wk: 21–30% FiO2
7. Thermal Management in the Delivery Room
7.1 Physiology of Heat Loss
Fetus: maintains body temp ~0.5°C higher than maternal temp; brown fat accumulation (late gestation) enables neonatal thermogenesis via oxidative metabolism
ELBW: no brown fat + large surface:mass ratio + asphyxia → markedly impaired thermoregulation
Consequences of hypothermia: metabolic acidosis, ↑ O2 consumption, ↑ mortality
Laptook AR Pediatrics 2007 (large cohort study): 28% ↑ mortality per 1°C decrease in NICU admission temperature in VLBW infants
>30% of ELBW (<1000g) become hypothermic with standard techniques (Bhatt 2010)
7.2 Strategies by Gestational Age

rows: Skin-to-skin with mother; radiant warmer if not vigorous; dry thoroughly; remove wet blankets
Radiant warmer; dry well; prewarmed blankets; hat; heated humidified gas for ventilation

Preheat DR to 75–78°F; food-grade polyethylene plastic wrap (do NOT dry first); exothermic mattress; hat; heated humidified gas. HeLP trial: Vohra J Pediatr 2004 — plastic wrap significantly reduces heat loss

Normothermia target 36.5–37.5°C; avoid hyperthermia — animal studies: hyperthermia + asphyxia synergistic → worsened cerebral injury. If HIE criteria met → therapeutic hypothermia 33–34°C (distinct protocol)

blocks.text: 8. Monitoring and Assessment During Resuscitation
8.1 Heart Rate Monitoring
Palpation of pulse (umbilical cord, peripheral): NOT reliable (Dawson Semin Fetal Neonatal Med 2018) → auscultation better but still inaccurate under stress
ECG leads: preferred method for HR monitoring — accurate, immediate; attach early if anticipated difficult resuscitation or VLBW
Pulse oximeter: 1–2 min delay before accurate readings; may fail if poor perfusion or very low HR; apply to right wrist/palm (preductal)
8.2 Tactile Stimulation
Drying alone is sufficient in most cases; gently flick soles or rub back as additional stimulation
After initial steps: if no vigorous response → assume secondary apnea → PPV without further delay
8.3 Oxygen Monitoring — Pulse Oximetry
Indications for pulse oximeter: when resuscitation anticipated, when PPV given, when central cyanosis persists after 5–10 min
All hospitals should be capable of O2 blending + pulse oximetry in delivery room
Color assessment unreliable; SpO2 provides objective data for O2 titration
9. Oxygen Use in Neonatal Resuscitation

Oxygen Guidelines — NRP 2020 (NLS ILCOR CoSTR 2020)
≥35 wk GA: start PPV with 21% O2 (room air); titrate up with SpO2 target table. <35 wk GA: start with 21–30% FiO2; avoid both hypoxia and hyperoxia — ROP, BPD, oxidative stress risk in preterm. Chest compressions or epinephrine needed: 100% O2. Blow-by oxygen: NOT indicated for transient cyanosis — normal term infant may take 6+ min to reach SpO2 >90%. Avoid high O2 in preterm: hyperoxia + prematurity → ↑ oxidative injury; use blender. If spontaneously breathing + HR >100 but cyanotic >5 min: free-flow O2 until SaO2 >85% or rule out cyanotic CHD.
Evidence: Lakshminrusimha Pediatr Res 2007: air resuscitation ↓ PVR 72% at 30 min vs 100% O2 group — equivalent at 60–90 min; O2 not essential for PVR reduction
Welsford M Pediatrics 2019 (meta-analysis): room air for term newborn initiation → supported; 100% O2 → ↑ mortality
Oei JL Arch Dis Child 2017 (meta-analysis, <28 wk): higher vs lower O2 in DR: no significant difference in survival/BPD; current 21–30% guideline remains best evidence-based practice
10. Apgar Score and Umbilical Cord Blood Gases
10.1 Apgar Score

Apgar Score — ข้อห้ามใช้เป็น Primary Indicator of Resuscitation
5 criteria: Heart rate (0/1/>100), Respiratory effort (0/slow/<2/cry), Muscle tone (0/some flexion/active), Reflex irritability (0/grimace/cry), Color (blue/acrocyanotic/pink). ข้อจำกัดสำคัญ: (1) ไม่ assign จนกว่า 1 min → too late to initiate resuscitation. (2) Asphyxia อาจ start in utero → ไม่สามารถรอ Apgar. (3) Subjective (especially color/tone in preterm). Correct use: define condition at set time points (1, 5, 10, 15, 20 min); guide ongoing resuscitation pace; document in medical record. If Apgar <7 at 5 min → reassess every 5 min until ≥7 (AAP 2015).
10.2 Umbilical Cord Blood Gas
Indications (ACOG recommendation): low 5-min Apgar, severe IUGR, FHR tracing abnormalities, intrapartum fever, multiple gestation, CS for fetal distress
Arterial cord gas = fetal acid-base state at time of delivery; adverse neurologic sequelae associated with low arterial cord pH (King J Pediatr 1998)
Normal umbilical cord blood gas values:
How to confirm arterial sample: arterial pH should be ≥0.02 lower than venous; PCO2 should be ≥4 mmHg higher than venous. If difference ≤0.02 → likely both from same vessel (probably vein)
Clue: PaO2 >31 mm Hg → likely venous sample (umbilical vein larger, easier to sample)
HIE cooling criteria: arterial cord pH <7.0 + base excess >−16 + signs of neonatal encephalopathy → therapeutic hypothermia (Jacobs Cochrane 2013; Papile Pediatrics 2014)
11. Ethical and Legal Aspects
11.1 Legal Framework
Child Abuse Amendments 1984: required physicians to provide care EXCEPT: (1) infant clinically and irreversibly comatose, (2) treatment would only prolong dying, (3) treatment deemed 'futile'
1989: civil rights law mandated medical care for every child; criminal + civil liability in cases of non-resuscitation
No law mandating resuscitation in ALL circumstances; local/state laws differ
11.2 Periviable Counseling
Parents must be part of decision-making — especially periviable infant; prenatal consultation by neonatologist critical when time allows
AMA code of medical ethics: consider chance of success, risk of treatment/non-treatment, quality of life with/without treatment, pain/suffering
When parental direction is unclear: provider must be advocate for the child
Family-centered care: parents may be present during resuscitation; informed + involved within FCC principles (Harrison Pediatrics 1993)
12. Algorithm — NRP 2020 Decision Tree (Bedside Reference)

rows: Skin-to-skin with mother; routine care; ongoing observation; no suctioning
Radiant warmer; Warm, Dry (or plastic wrap if <28 wk), Stimulate, Position (sniff); suction if secretions
At ~1 min: Apnea/gasping OR HR <100 bpm
PPV + SpO2 probe (right hand) ± cardiac monitor. FiO2: ≥35 wk = 21%; <35 wk = 21–30%
Position airway, suction if needed, consider CPAP, supplemental O2 if SpO2 below target
Ensure adequate ventilation (MR SOPA); consider ETT or laryngeal mask airway
ETT + chest compressions (3:1 ratio) + 100% O2 + UVC
IV epinephrine 0.01–0.03 mg/kg q3–5 min; consider NS 10 mL/kg if hypovolemia; consider pneumothorax
After every resuscitation — identify team communication errors; review video if recorded

blocks.text: 13. NICU Practical — เตรียมรับทารกหลัง Delivery Room Resuscitation
Post-resuscitation monitoring: ทารกที่ต้องการ chest compressions/epinephrine มีความเสี่ยงสูงต่อ HIE, myocardial dysfunction, PPHN, renal injury, NEC —
HIE workup: cord pH/BE, cooling criteria assessment, aEEG/seizure monitoring
Cardiac: echo for myocardial function/PPHN; troponin; ECG
Renal: UO monitoring, creatinine day 2–3
GI: NPO initially; trophic feeding cautiously if hemodynamically stable; NEC vigilance
Preterm resuscitated in DR: admission temperature target 36.5–37.5°C; document DR temperature in medical record
Meconium-stained AF + depressed infant: intubate and suction trachea ONLY if not vigorous; vigorous infant with meconium → routine care (no suction trachea); see Ch.33 for full protocol

Narcotic-depressed infant: mother received opioids <4h before delivery; PPV first; naloxone 0.1 mg/kg IM/IV ONLY after HR established with PPV — not a 'first step'; avoid if maternal substance abuse (withdrawal risk in neonate)
Golden hour protocol (ELBW): assigned team roles, scripted communication, pre-drawn medications, UVC ready before delivery — ↓ chaos, ↑ hypothermia prevention, ↑ surfactant timing accuracy
Parental update: ให้ข้อมูล parents ทันทีหลัง resuscitation เสถียร — FCC principle; ถ้า parents present during resus → debrief กับพวกเขาด้วย
14. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — Ventilation = the Intervention: Chest Compressions Come Last
นี่คือความต่างที่สำคัญที่สุดระหว่าง neonatal resuscitation กับ adult ACLS: ทารกเกือบทั้งหมดที่ต้องการ resuscitation ตอบสนองต่อ adequate PPV เพียงอย่างเดียว. Chest compressions เริ่มต้นก็ต่อเมื่อ HR <60 แม้ adequate ventilation. ดังนั้น: ถ้า resuscitation ไม่ได้ผล → ถามตัวเองก่อนว่า 'ventilation adequate หรือเปล่า?' ก่อนขยับไปขั้นต่อไป. MR SOPA (Mask-Reposition-Suction-Open mouth-Pressure up-Airway alt) = troubleshooting checklist สำหรับ ineffective PPV.

PEARL 2 — Assume Secondary Apnea: Never Wait to See if Stimulation Works
ไม่มีวิธีแยก primary จาก secondary apnea ในห้อง delivery ณ เวลานั้น. ยิ่งรอนาน → ยิ่ง time to first spontaneous gasp นานขึ้นหลัง PPV (Adamsons 1964). กฎ: depressed neonate ใดๆ = secondary apnea จนกว่าจะพิสูจน์ตรงข้าม → PPV ทันที. อย่า 'stimulate ก่อนแล้วรอดู' ถ้า baby ดู depressed จริงๆ — นั่นคือ การทำให้ hypoxia ยาวนานขึ้น.

PEARL 3 — SpO2 ต่ำที่ 1–2 min คือ NORMAL: อย่า rush ให้ O2
Term infant SpO2 1 min หลังเกิด อาจอยู่ที่ 60–65% — นั่นคือ target ตาม NRP 2020. Blow-by oxygen สำหรับ transient cyanosis = outdated practice และ ไม่ได้ผล. ทารก term ปกติใช้เวลาหลายนาทีกว่าจะ SpO2 >90% — นั่นเป็น normal transition. มีข้อมูลว่าการให้ 100% O2 ใน term infant → ↑ mortality vs room air (Welsford 2019). ใช้ pulse oximeter แทนการดู color; titrate O2 to target.

PEARL 4 — ELBW + Cold = Preventable Mortality: Plastic Wrap ก่อน Dry
สำหรับทารก <28 wk (<1000g): ห้ามเช็ดตัวก่อน — ให้ใส่ plastic wrap ทันทีที่คลอด ขณะยังเปียกอยู่. Vohra HeLP trial (J Pediatr 2004): plastic wrap ลด heat loss ได้ significant. Laptook 2007: 28% ↑ mortality per 1°C decrease in admission temperature. Preheat delivery room to 75–78°F; exothermic mattress ใต้ warmer; heated humidified gas; hat; ต้าน FOUR sources of heat loss: evaporation, conduction, convection, radiation.

PEARL 5 — HANDS ก่อนมาถึง: Preparation = Outcome
การ resuscitation ที่วุ่นวาย ส่วนใหญ่เกิดจาก: late notification, unavailable equipment, unskilled staff, poor coordination — ไม่ใช่ clinical difficulty. HANDS mnemonic ใช้ตอนรับ call: H=Hemorrhage, A=Amniotic fluid, N=Number, D=Dates, S=Strip. ข้อมูลนี้กำหนด team size, equipment (UVC/epi drawn?), cord clamping plan, DCC vs immediate. สำหรับ ELBW: 'scripted golden hour' protocol กับ assigned roles ก่อน delivery ↓ chaos + ↑ hypothermia prevention + ↑ early surfactant.
15. Evidence Update — Key Studies
Weiner GM ed. NRP Textbook 8th ed AAP/AHA 2020: current US guidelines; derived from ILCOR CoSTR 2020; NRP algorithm, SpO2 targets, oxygen use, cord management, initial steps reordered
Wyckoff MH Resuscitation 2020;156:A156 (ILCOR CoSTR 2020): international consensus on NLS; foundation for NRP 8th ed; treatment recommendations may differ by country

Perlman JM & Risser R Arch Pediatr Adolesc Med 1995;149:20: cardiopulmonary resuscitation in delivery room: ~3% newborns require PPV immediately after birth; ~1–2/1000 require full resuscitation (chest compressions/meds)

Hooper SB et al Arch Dis Child Fetal Neonatal Ed 2016;101:F266 (three-phase respiratory transition): seminal description of 3-phase process; basis for understanding why sustained inflation and PEEP may help in apneic newborn
Kirpalani H et al JAMA 2019;321:1165 (SAIL trial): SLI vs IPPV in <26 wk gestation; STOPPED EARLY due to ↑ mortality in SLI group → sustained inflation NOT recommended for preterm at birth

Lakshminrusimha S et al Pediatr Res 2007;62:313: PVR in lambs: air resuscitation ↓ PVR 72% at 30 min; equivalent to 100% O2 at 60–90 min — ventilation alone sufficient for PVR reduction; O2 not essential early
Laptook AR et al Pediatrics 2007;119:e643: admission temperature predictors in LBW: 28% ↑ mortality per 1°C decrease — most powerful thermal management evidence

Vohra S et al J Pediatr 2004;145:750 (HeLP trial): polyethylene occlusive skin wrapping in very preterm: significantly reduces heat loss; HR + temp improved; established plastic wrap as standard practice
Welsford M et al Pediatrics 2019;143:e20181825 (meta-analysis): room air (21%) for term newborn initiation: supported; 100% O2 associated with ↑ mortality → basis for current 21% recommendation

Oei JL et al Arch Dis Child Fetal Neonatal Ed 2017;102:F24 (meta-analysis, <28 wk): higher vs lower O2 for DR resuscitation preterm <28 wk: no significant survival/BPD difference; current 21–30% guideline reflects best available data
ACOG Committee Opinion No. 684 (2017): delayed cord clamping (DCC) 30–60 sec for vigorous term + preterm: ↑ blood volume, ↓ inotropic support, ↓ IVH, ↓ NEC — current standard recommendation
Adamsons K Jr et al J Pediatr 1964;65:807: rhesus monkey model: asphyxia duration correlated linearly with time-to-spontaneous-breathing after PPV → 'every minute counts' physiologic basis
Jacobs SE et al Cochrane 2013;1:CD003311: therapeutic hypothermia for HIE: ↓ death/neurodisability; foundation for cooling protocol
Foster JP et al Cochrane 2017;4:CD010332: routine oropharyngeal suction vs no suction: no benefit for vigorous babies born through clear fluid — supports current 'no routine suction' recommendation
Dawson JA et al Semin Fetal Neonatal Med 2018;23:327: monitoring HR in delivery room: pulse palpation + auscultation unreliable; ECG preferred for accurate HR assessment
16. Self-Check Q&A
Q: อะไรคือหลักการสำคัญที่แยก neonatal resuscitation จาก adult ACLS?

ตอบ: Ventilation = the primary intervention. Adult ACLS เน้น circulation (compressions) ก่อน; neonatal resuscitation: เกือบทุก depressed neonate ตอบสนองต่อ adequate ventilation (PPV) อย่างเดียว. Chest compressions เริ่มเฉพาะเมื่อ HR <60 แม้ adequate ventilation แล้ว. สาเหตุ: neonatal cardiac arrest = respiratory origin; adult cardiac arrest = primary cardiac. ดังนั้นถ้า resus ไม่ได้ผล → troubleshoot ventilation ก่อนขยับไป compression เสมอ.
Q: ทำไมถึงต้อง assume secondary apnea เสมอในทารกที่ดู depressed?

ตอบ: Primary vs secondary apnea ไม่สามารถแยกได้จาก clinical appearance. Primary apnea: ตอบสนองต่อ stimulation; secondary apnea: ต้องการ PPV. เนื่องจากไม่รู้ว่า apnea เริ่มก่อน delivery นานเท่าไหร่ → ต้อง treat as secondary apnea ทันที. Adamsons 1964: ยิ่ง asphyxia ยาว → ยิ่งนานกว่าจะ spontaneous breathing หลัง PPV → delay = harm. Rule: depressed neonate = secondary apnea until proven otherwise.
Q: NRP 2020: เริ่ม O2 ที่ FiO2 เท่าไหร่ในทารก ≥35 wk vs <35 wk?

ตอบ: ≥35 wk: 21% (room air) เสมอ. <35 wk: 21–30% FiO2. Titrate ตาม SpO2 target: 1 min = 60–65%, 2 min = 65–70%, 3 min = 70–75%, 4 min = 75–80%, 5 min = 80–85%, 10 min = 85–95%. Chest compressions/epinephrine needed → 100% O2. Blow-by oxygen for transient cyanosis = ไม่ recommended. Evidence: Welsford 2019 (term air = ↓ mortality); Oei 2017 (preterm 21–30% = current best evidence).
Q: ELBW <28 wk: thermal management ใน DR ต้องทำอะไรบ้าง?

ตอบ: (1) Preheat DR to 75–78°F; (2) Plastic wrap (food-grade polyethylene): ใส่ทันทีที่คลอดขณะยังเปียก — ห้าม dry ก่อน; (3) Exothermic mattress ใต้ radiant warmer; (4) Hat; (5) Heated humidified O2/air for ventilation. Evidence: Vohra HeLP 2004 (plastic wrap ↓ heat loss); Laptook 2007 (28% ↑ mortality per 1°C ↓ admission temp). Note: avoid hyperthermia in asphyxiated infants — synergistic injury with asphyxia.
Q: Umbilical cord blood gas: อย่างไรจึงรู้ว่า sample เป็น arterial vs venous?

ตอบ: Arterial: pH ≥0.02 lower than venous; PCO2 ≥4 mmHg higher than venous. ถ้า difference ≤0.02 pH units หรือ <4 mmHg PCO2 → likely both from same vessel (vein). Clue: PaO2 >31 mmHg = likely venous sample (umbilical vein larger → easier to sample accidentally). HIE cooling criteria require arterial cord pH <7.0 + BE >−16 → ต้อง confirm arterial origin before making cooling decision.
Q: HANDS mnemonic ใช้อย่างไรและทำไมสำคัญ?

ตอบ: H=Hemorrhage, A=Amniotic fluid, N=Number, D=Dates, S=Strip. ใช้เมื่อรับ emergency call จาก OB team เพื่อ gather ข้อมูลสำคัญก่อน arrive: (1) H → UVC/epi ต้อง ready? (2) A → meconium = prepare intubation. (3) N → twin/triplet = extra team. (4) D → GA = ขนาด blade/ETT/O2 protocol. (5) S → Category III = prepare full resuscitation. Preparation = outcome: ส่วนใหญ่ของ chaotic resuscitation เกิดจาก poor preparation ไม่ใช่ clinical complexity.
Q: Apgar score: ใช้ได้เมื่อไหร่ และไม่ควรใช้อย่างไร?

ตอบ: ใช้ได้: define condition at 1, 5, 10, 15, 20 min; document resuscitation progress; guide pace of resuscitation. ไม่ควรใช้เป็น trigger ของ resuscitation — 1-min Apgar not assigned until 1 min → too late; asphyxia อาจ start in utero. Apgar 0–3 at 1 min = often secondary apnea. Apgar <7 at 5 min → reassess every 5 min until ≥7 (AAP 2015). Subjective (color/tone in preterm). ไม่ใช้เป็น sole criterion สำหรับ therapeutic hypothermia — ต้องใช้ cord pH + clinical HIE criteria ร่วม.
Q: Periviable infant: กรอบ ethical ในการตัดสินใจ resuscitate หรือ withhold คืออะไร?

ตอบ: (1) AMA code: พิจารณา chance of success, risk of treatment, quality of life, pain/suffering. (2) Child Abuse Amendments 1984: ต้องให้ care ยกเว้น irreversibly comatose / treatment only prolongs dying / futile. (3) Parents must be informed + involved — especially periviable infant; prenatal neonatology consultation is key. (4) When parental direction unclear → provider = child's advocate. (5) Local/state laws differ → know your jurisdiction's rules. Goal: shared decision-making with family, documented, before delivery when possible.

Day 128 Preview — Fanaroff Ch.31
บทต่อไป: Chapter 31 — Role of Positive Pressure Ventilation in Neonatal Resuscitation. เตรียมตัว: T-piece resuscitator vs self-inflating bag vs flow-inflating bag; optimal peak inspiratory pressure (PIP) + PEEP settings; MR SOPA troubleshooting checklist; laryngeal mask airway (LMA) indication + technique; CPAP at birth สำหรับ preterm; CO2 detector confirmation of ETT position; criteria สำหรับ intubation vs mask PPV; evidence for sustained inflation vs standard PPV.
